gates.name: Strong Signal Detection
gates.criterion: The system can distinguish stronger candidate signals from weaker or noisier evidence.

gates.name: Signal Routing
gates.criterion: Live signals can be translated into standardized review-oriented artifacts.

gates.name: Review and Inbox Bridge
gates.criterion: Stronger signals do not vanish informally; they can enter a review/inbox path.

gates.name: Adoption Candidate Path
gates.criterion: Candidate states such as proposed/pending/adopted/parked exist with enough structure to evaluate one-path adoption.

gates.name: Adoption Mechanism
gates.criterion: A canonical adoption object and state machine can move one reviewed signal into one reversible runtime-affecting change.

gates.name: Runtime Change Traceability
gates.criterion: The system can show exactly what changed, when it changed, and which adoption object caused it.

gates.name: Measured Effect Verification
gates.criterion: At least one documented case links a reviewed signal to a change and then to a later measured effect with explicit uncertainty.

gates.name: Repeated Closed Loops
gates.criterion: More than one closed-loop case works without bespoke manual glue each time.
